changes.reason: Call-wall owns the current -655.2 MAE tail and a 120-minute hold; lowering the adaptive cap from 6 to the grid value 5 retests the historically positive realization-containment direction without disabling the rare convex profile.

The worst call-wall winner used an effective target near 20.6 points and only resolved after deep heat. A 4.5 fixed distance is the least restrictive grid cap and should force earlier realization while preserving some call-wall upside.

Raise acceptance persistence from 20 to 45 seconds so transient call-wall touches are less likely to enter the wide-target recovery path; this stays within the call-wall acceptance family and keeps valid-0DTE confirmation intact.

recommendation.expected_effect: Should reduce call_wall_acceptance_long hold time, selected TP distance, and worst/average MAE contribution while preserving the profile's positive expectancy. Main tradeoff is underpaying the single largest convex winner; reject if total net profit falls more than 2% versus run_001/run_044 or if retained call-wall PnL is not offset by better risk-adjusted score.
recommendation.label: call-wall-realization-box
recommendation.rationale: Use batch_a_call_wall_acceptance_long as a surgical profile-specific realization test. The current tail is not a broad entry-quality problem: the worst trade is a valid-0DTE call-wall acceptance winner with strong forward DEX but extreme effective target, 120-minute hold, and -655.2 MAE. Prior broad extended-VWAP veto widening hurt score and did not improve worst MAE, while call-wall TP containment was historically mildly positive. This changes only the call-wall acceptance family and avoids global entry tightening.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: call_wall_acceptance_long produced only 1 run_056 trade but $546 PnL and the portfolio worst MAE of -655.2 with a 20.95 configured/effective TP path. Static admission pruning risks deleting the biggest winner, so the cleaner test is earlier realization plus longer wall acceptance persistence.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Call-Wall Early Realization Box
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Small one-trade sample; a tighter target can suppress rare convex payout without materially improving portfolio MAE if another profile becomes the new tail.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: VALID_0DTE call-wall acceptance with positive forward DEX and accepted strike context, but wide selected target and extended ES-VWAP state.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Worst MAE improves below -655.2 or average MAE/exposure improves, call_wall_acceptance_long retains at least 70% PnL contribution, total net profit remains >=98% of run_044, completed trades drop <=10%, and pnl_per_exposure_hour stays flat or better.

strategy_hypotheses.evidence: The worst call-wall trade made early favorable progress but reached full TP only after deep adverse excursion; current config lacks a direct path-aware way to distinguish this from clean convex continuation.
strategy_hypotheses.name: Call-Wall Convex Recovery Monitor
strategy_hypotheses.risk: Can overfit a one- or two-trade profile and cut the rare winner unless validated against protected fast winners and out-of-sample days.
strategy_hypotheses.trigger: call_wall_acceptance_long entry with wide target or high ES-VWAP extension that fails to convert early favorable movement into sustained continuation.
strategy_hypotheses.validation_metric: Reduce call-wall worst MAE materially while retaining >=70% call-wall PnL, total net profit >=98% of leader, protected fast-winner retention >=95%, and slow_12h remains 0.
